items.question: It ___ that the company is in trouble.
explanations: Correct! Impersonal pattern: 'It is said that…'.
'It is said to' needs a 'to'-infinitive, not 'that'.
'It says that' is active and changes the meaning.
'It said that' misses the passive 'is'.

items.question: The company ___ in trouble. (personal pattern)
explanations: Correct! Personal pattern: 'The company is said to be in trouble'.
'Is said that' belongs to the impersonal 'It' pattern.
'Says to be' is active and ungrammatical.
'Is said being' is not correct English.

items.question: He ___ the country last night. (an earlier action)
explanations: 'To leave' points to now; the action is earlier.
Correct! Earlier time = to have + participle: 'is believed to have left'.
'To have left' is needed, not '-ing'.
'Believes to leave' is active and wrong.

items.question: It ___ that no one was injured in the crash.
explanations: Correct! Impersonal past: 'It was reported that…'.
'It was reported to' needs an infinitive, not 'that'.
'It reported that' is active.
'Is reporting' is active continuous.

items.question: She ___ five languages fluently.
explanations: Correct! Personal pattern: 'She is known to speak five languages'.
'Is known that speaks' mixes the two patterns.
'Knows to speak' is active and changes the meaning.
'Is known speaking' is not correct.

items.question: Transform: 'People think he is a genius.' (personal pattern)
options: He is thought to be a genius.
He is thought that he is a genius.
It is thought he to be a genius.
He thinks to be a genius.
explanations: Correct! 'He is thought to be a genius'.
'Is thought that he is' mixes patterns.
'It is thought he to be' is ungrammatical.
'He thinks to be' changes the meaning.

items.question: They ___ a fortune in the 1990s. (earlier action, personal)
explanations: 'To make' points to now; the 1990s is earlier.
Correct! Earlier time: 'are said to have made a fortune'.
'-ing' doesn't fit this pattern.
'Say to have made' is active and wrong.

items.question: Correct this: 'He is said that he is very rich.'
options: He is said to be very rich.
He is said to being very rich.
He said to be very rich.
explanations: Correct! Personal pattern: 'He is said to be very rich'.
'To being' is wrong; use 'to be'.
'He said to be' misses the passive 'is'.
The original wrongly mixes 'He is said' with a 'that'-clause.

items.question: Transform to impersonal: 'The minister is expected to resign.'
options: It is expected that the minister will resign.
It is expected the minister to resign.
It expects that the minister resigns.
The minister is expected that resigns.
explanations: Correct! Impersonal: 'It is expected that the minister will resign'.
'It is expected the minister to resign' is ungrammatical.
'It expects that' is active.
'Is expected that resigns' mixes the patterns.

items.question: Which sentence correctly reports an EARLIER action?
options: The painting is believed to be stolen.
The painting is believed to have been stolen.
The painting believes to be stolen.
explanations: 'To be stolen' points to now, not an earlier theft.
Correct! Earlier passive action: 'is believed to have been stolen'.
'-ing' doesn't fit this pattern.
'Believes to be' is active and wrong.
